questionText: A particle has position $x = t^2$, $y = 3t$ (metres) at time $t$ seconds. Find the horizontal and vertical velocity components at $t = 2$.

steps.description: Recall that velocity is the rate of change of position
steps.explanation: In a motion model, differentiating each position component with respect to time gives the corresponding velocity component.
The horizontal velocity is the derivative of $x = t^2$.
The vertical velocity is the derivative of $y = 3t$, which is constant.
Substituting $t = 2$ gives the two velocity components.
These are the horizontal and vertical velocities at $t = 2$ s.
